title: Anthemis urvilleana
taxonomy: Anthemis urvilleana is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is classified as a member of the genus Anthemis. Within the genus Anthemis, it belongs to the section Anthemis. The species is further classified as a member of the subsection Anthemis. Its classification follows the Linnaean system of taxonomy.
uses: Anthemis urvilleana is known to have been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. Its leaves and flowers have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Anthemis urvilleana for medicinal purposes. However, its traditional uses suggest potential benefits in the treatment of certain health conditions.
benefits: Anthemis urvilleana, also known as Chrysocoma or Urvillean chamomile, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. Its flowers are used in herbal medicine to treat digestive issues and soothe skin irritations. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling and protect against cell damage. Anthemis urvilleana may also be used to calm anxiety and promote relaxation. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
properties: Anthemis urvilleana, also known as the pyrethrum daisy, has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties. Research suggests that the plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may be beneficial for wound healing and skin conditions. Additionally, Anthemis urvilleana has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever and digestive problems. However, more scientific studies are needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using this plant for therapeutic purposes. Further research may uncover more about its potential health benefits and applications.
constituents: Anthemis urvilleana, a plant in the daisy family, contains various active constituents. These constituents include flavonoids, such as luteolin, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, Anthemis urvilleana may also contain sesquiterpene lactones, like costunolide, which are often found in plants of the Compositae family. The exact composition of Anthemis urvilleana's active constituents may vary depending on factors such as the plant's habitat and growing conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's bioactive properties.
parts: Anthemis urvilleana. The medicinal parts of Anthemis urvilleana are not well-documented, but it is believed to have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. preparations: Anthemis urvilleana is a species of Anthemis, but no information is available on herbal preparations of this specific plant. However, Anthemis urvilleana is part of the daisy family and its close relatives, like Calendula and Chamaemelum, are used in herbal preparations for their medicinal properties. intro: Anthemis urvilleana, also known as Urvillean chamomile, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing effects, making it useful in treating anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Anthemis urvilleana include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and volatile oils, particularly apigenin and luteolin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Urvillean chamomile is commonly used in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to create sleep-promoting and stress-relieving formulations.
